 {"id":"bd-caex2","title":"[MOCK-FINDER] btrfs_ino_lookup only supports root objectid; non-root returns ENOENT with 'not implemented yet' comment","description":"# Mock-finder audit finding

`FsOps::btrfs_ino_lookup` at crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16062-16082 only returns a path for `objectid == BTRFS_FIRST_FREE_OBJECTID (256)` and falls through to `Err(FfsError::Io(libc::ENOENT))` for every other inode, with the comment: `// Full back-reference walking not implemented yet.`

## Context

The prior bead bd-jfsga ('add BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP ioctl support') was closed 2026-04-21 as 'done' with acceptance criterion 'BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP returns correct path for known inodes'. The current implementation satisfies that criterion only for the single well-known root objectid; every other known inode returns ENOENT indistinguishable from 'inode does not exist'. Callers (btrfs-progs, user scripts) cannot tell the difference between 'this inode genuinely doesn't exist' and 'this file doesn't implement lookup for non-root inodes'.

## Evidence

- Backend: crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16062-16082
- FUSE dispatcher wiring: crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:2100-2125 (correctly routes the ioctl)
- Comment verbatim: 'Full back-reference walking not implemented yet.'

## Why this is a real stub, not an intentional scope limit

Unlike write-participating MMP (crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:6531) and e2compr lzv1/lzrw3a/bzip2 (6989, 7955), which surface a bespoke `UnsupportedFeature` error with a clear scope message, this path returns a generic ENOENT that masks the limitation from callers and from conformance tools.

## Fix direction

Walk the btrfs INODE_REF / INODE_EXTREF back-references chain from the target objectid up to the tree root, assembling path components in reverse. Reference implementation: fs/btrfs/ioctl.c::btrfs_search_path_in_tree in Linux v6.19. For v1 scope, supporting only same-subvolume paths (no cross-subvolume) is acceptable if documented.

## Acceptance

- Non-root objectid that exists in the subvolume tree returns its reconstructed path.
- Non-existent objectid returns a structured NotFound / FfsError that the FUSE layer maps to ENOENT (current behavior preserved for truly-missing inodes).
- Unit + mounted-path test that resolves a file created at runtime and asserts the returned path matches.
- If cross-subvolume resolution is out of scope, emit `UnsupportedFeature('cross-subvolume btrfs_ino_lookup not implemented')` with a distinct errno mapping instead of ENOENT.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:17:45.327464791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:49:18.168402424Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:49:18.166600714Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit f70dcca. btrfs_ino_lookup (crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16051) now walks INODE_REF back-references from the target objectid up to the subvolume root, assembling a /-separated path with trailing / before the terminating NUL (matches fs/btrfs/ioctl.c::btrfs_search_path_in_tree). Algorithm extracted as pure helper btrfs_build_path_from_inode_refs for unit-testability; 6 new tests cover subvolume root, single hop, multi-hop a/b/c/, missing back-ref → NotFound, cycle → Corruption, and hard-link first-wins determinism. Cross-subvolume (ROOT_REF) remains out-of-V1-scope but now returns UnsupportedFeature with a clear message rather than silent ENOENT. All 6 new tests + existing ffs-core suite pass via rch; workspace clippy green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cb0kd","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_readdir_image still computes invalid tree_block_csum after explicit stamping","description":"# Review finding

The current `build_btrfs_readdir_image()` fixture already writes explicit CRC32C values into both synthetic tree blocks, but `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` still fails the six btrfs directory-walk tests with `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`.

## Evidence
- Builder already stamps both blocks: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23225-23378`.
- It computes `csum_root` and `csum_fs` over `image[tree + 0x20 .. tree + nodesize]` and writes them to bytes `0..4`.
- After `br-bd-o5114 repair btrfs default-root smoke fixture`, the remaining red frontier is exactly:
  - `walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
  - `btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
  - `btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
  - `btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
  - `btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
  - `btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`
- All six still fail on the same checksum mismatch, so the root cause is no longer "missing stamp calls".

## Why this is high severity
This keeps the full workspace test gate red and shows the older broad review bead is now under-specified. The next implementation pass needs to debug the actual checksum semantics, not just add stamp writes.

## Expected fix direction
Audit the exact checksum window, node header fields, and final byte mutations for the 16 KiB synthetic readdir fixture. Verify the bytes being hashed match what the parser later validates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:53Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","close_reason":"Already shipped; targeted btrfs readdir repros now pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E proves empty listxattr size-probe and zero-length payload behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:20:55.963753985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:20:08.471473942Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:20:08.469626255Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cebm","title":"Implement FUSE fsync/flush with durability guarantees","description":"# Implement FUSE fsync/flush with durability guarantees

## GOAL
Implement correct fsync and flush semantics for FUSE mount, ensuring durability guarantees match user expectations.

## BACKGROUND
fsync is the primary durability contract:
- `fsync(fd)` must ensure all data written to fd is on stable storage
- `fdatasync(fd)` same but may skip non-essential metadata
- `flush` is called on close (cleanup, not durability)

## CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. fsync Semantics
```rust
impl FuseBackend {
    fn fsync(&self, ino: u64, fh: u64, datasync: bool, reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty) {
        // 1. Get inode from fh
        // 2. Commit any pending MVCC transaction for this file
        // 3. Flush dirty cache entries for this inode's blocks
        // 4. If !datasync: also flush inode metadata block
        // 5. Wait for I/O completion
        // 6. If repair_eager: refresh RaptorQ symbols for dirty groups
        // 7. reply.ok() or reply.error(errno)
    }
}
```

### 2. Flush Semantics
```rust
impl FuseBackend {
    fn flush(&self, ino: u64, fh: u64, lock_owner: u64, reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty) {
        // Called on close - release resources, no durability guarantee
        // 1. Release file handle
        // 2. Decrement open count
        // 3. If last close + unlinked: queue for deletion
        // 4. reply.ok()
    }
}
```

### 3. fsyncdir Semantics
```rust
impl FuseBackend {
    fn fsyncdir(&self, ino: u64, fh: u64, datasync: bool, reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty) {
        // Same as fsync but for directories
        // Ensures directory entries are durable
    }
}
```

### 4. Integration Points
- **MVCC**: fsync triggers transaction commit
- **ARC Cache**: fsync flushes dirty entries for inode
- **RaptorQ**: fsync may trigger eager symbol refresh
- **Journal**: fsync ensures journal commit block written

### 5. Error Handling
- I/O error during fsync -> EROFS or EIO
- MVCC conflict -> EAGAIN (retry)
- Timeout -> EIO with detailed logging

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: fsync flushes dirty blocks for inode
2. Unit: fdatasync skips metadata
3. Integration: fsync + crash + recovery -> data durable
4. Integration: fsync on RO mount -> EROFS
5. E2E: Write + fsync + unmount + remount -> data present

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- fsync start (debug): inode, datasync flag
- fsync cache flush (trace): block count, dirty bytes
- fsync commit (trace): commit_seq
- fsync complete (debug): duration_us, blocks_flushed
- fsync error (warn): inode, error, context

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. fsync guarantees data is on stable storage
2. fsync failure returns correct errno
3. No data loss if process crashes after successful fsync
4. Performance: fsync of single small file < 100ms","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:10.359139338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:58:39.214188659Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:58:39.214168702Z","close_reason":"Verified fsync/flush/fsyncdir durability semantics and focused rch test evidence; criteria satisfied","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","durability","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cebm","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:18.909563807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cebm","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.663794504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-cebm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:21Z"},{"id":208,"issue_id":"bd-cebm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by Codex. Starting implementation now. Planned touch set: crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs, crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs, crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs tests as needed. Please avoid overlapping edits.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:39Z"},{"id":210,"issue_id":"bd-cebm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (Codex): implemented FUSE flush/fsync/fsyncdir path with durability plumbing. Added RequestOp variants, FsOps trait methods (default semantics), OpenFs ext4 durability sync via device sync, and ffs-fuse handlers for flush/fsync/fsyncdir with proper RO behavior and errno mapping. Added/updated unit tests in ffs-core and validated ffs-fuse tests. Full required gate chain passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:44:11Z"},{"id":212,"issue_id":"bd-cebm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (Codex): added durability proof/optimization slice. Code: strengthened write-back crash test with explicit fsync latency assertion (<100ms in-memory harness) in crates/ffs-block/tests/writeback_e2e.rs; added writeback fsync criterion benches in crates/ffs-block/benches/arc_cache.rs; extended scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh crash phase with explicit fsync marker vs non-fsync marker checks; wired benchmark script to run writeback fsync benches in scripts/benchmark.sh. Artifacts: artifacts/optimization/bd-cebm/{baseline_scenario3_fsync.json,strace_scenario3_fsync.txt,bench_writeback_sync_single_4k.txt,isomorphism_proof.md,evidence_ledger.jsonl,recommendation_contract.md,env.json,manifest.json,repro.lock}. Full gate chain passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace.","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:27:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-cigna","title":"Expand ext4 extent block checksum tests beyond round-trip + no-panic","description":"crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs has stamp/verify_extent_block_checksum, but the proptest coverage in the same file mirrors the gap that bd-anfg0 just closed for dir blocks:

  - ext4_proptest_verify_extent_block_checksum_no_panic (negative)
  - ext4_proptest_verify_extent_block_checksum_short_rejects (negative)
  - ext4_proptest_extent_block_checksum_stamp_verify (round-trip)

Apply the same four-relation MR template (MR-INO, MR-GEN, MR-SEED, MR-DETECT) so that extent block stamping is pinned at the same behavioural-contract level as inode (bd-z75f6) and dir block (bd-anfg0). The covered region for an extent block is `[0..tail_off]` where `tail_off = 12 + 12 * eh_max`; flips outside that range (in the 4 stored-checksum bytes) must be excluded from MR-DETECT.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude-frankenfs-pane","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:19:23.652242436Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:24:11.398687126Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T22:24:11.396342201Z","close_reason":"Added MR-INO, MR-GEN, MR-SEED, MR-DETECT proptests for extent block checksum, completing the ext4 metadata-checksum MR trio (inode, dir, extent). All 5 ext4_proptest_extent_block_checksum_* cases pass; clippy clean. sha=6c6dae6","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
